mnemonics: 3 Point, Find Fibonacci: For sequential search, remember '3 Point' is a simpler, fixed-interval method for unimodal functions, while 'Fibonacci' is an optimal, efficient method using the Fibonacci sequence to reduce intervals rapidly.
keyPoints: 3-Point Search evaluates three points (two endpoints and a midpoint) per iteration to reduce the search interval for finding a minimum.
Fibonacci Search uses the Fibonacci sequence to determine the optimal points for evaluation, minimizing the number of iterations required to achieve a desired precision.
Both methods assume the function is unimodal (has a single minimum) on the interval [a, b] and iteratively narrow down the interval containing the minimum.
commonMistakes: Applying these methods to non-unimodal functions, which can lead to convergence to a local minimum instead of the global minimum.
Incorrectly implementing the Fibonacci search by not properly updating the Fibonacci numbers or misplacing the evaluation points within the interval.
